incident_details.names_of_parties_involved_in_the_inciden: Unidentified male guest of Daniel Roberts

complaint.complaint_description:
I am submitting this complaint regarding ongoing excessive noise, late-night disturbances, and what appears to be frequent intoxicated behavior coming from my downstairs neighbor, Daniel Roberts in unit A-202, with the most recent and most serious incident occurring on the night of 2025-11-29 at approximately 11:45 PM.

I have lived in unit A-304 since March 2022 and generally have not had issues with other residents. However, beginning around August 2025, noise from unit A-202 has become a recurring problem, particularly on Thursday, Friday, and Saturday nights. The disturbances typically involve loud music with heavy bass, shouting and yelling from multiple people, doors slamming repeatedly, and people congregating in the hallway outside the unit late at night. On several occasions, I have also heard what sounded like arguments or people throwing objects.

On 2025-11-29, the situation escalated beyond what I consider normal or acceptable apartment noise. At about 10:30 PM, loud music started coming from A-202, with the bass so strong that my living room floor and coffee table were vibrating. I could hear lyrics clearly through my closed windows and doors. Around 11:00 PM, several additional people arrived, slamming the stairwell door and talking very loudly outside my door as they went down to A-202. The hallway smelled strongly of marijuana at that time.

By 11:30 PM, the noise level had increased further. I could hear multiple male voices shouting, what sounded like someone jumping or stomping directly under my bedroom, and loud bursts of laughter. I texted my upstairs neighbor, Sarah Kim in A-306, to confirm whether she was also hearing the disturbance. She responded that she could hear it clearly and that it had woken her up as well. We both have early work schedules and usually go to bed around 10:00 PM.

At approximately 11:45 PM, I heard a very loud crash from the direction of A-202, followed by someone yelling, "What is wrong with you?" and what sounded like glass breaking. Immediately afterward, there was loud banging on what I believe was the inside of their door and a lot of shouting. I could not make out every word, but I distinctly heard someone yell, "You can't just throw stuff like that," and another person respond with profanity. The situation sounded volatile enough that I felt uncomfortable leaving my apartment to ask them to quiet down directly.

This incident is not the first time I have been disturbed by noise from this unit. I have documented at least nine separate nights since 2025-08-15 when noise from A-202 has continued past 11:00 PM and interfered with my ability to sleep. I have kept a simple log on my phone noting dates and approximate times, including 2025-09-06, 2025-09-20, 2025-10-04, 2025-10-18, and 2025-11-09. On 2025-10-18, I knocked on Daniel's door around 10:45 PM and politely asked if they could turn the music down because I had to be up at 5:30 AM for work. Daniel was somewhat dismissive but did turn the volume down for about half an hour. However, the pattern of loud gatherings and late-night noise resumed the following weekends.

I work full-time as an IT support specialist and start my shift at 7:00 AM, which requires me to wake up early. The repeated late-night disturbances have affected my sleep, my ability to focus at work, and my general sense of comfort in my own home. I have resorted to wearing earplugs and using a white noise machine, but these measures do not fully block the bass vibrations and shouting when the gatherings in A-202 are particularly loud.

I want to emphasize that I understand apartment living involves some level of normal noise and that I am not expecting complete silence. I am mainly concerned about the frequency, intensity, and late hours of the disturbances, as well as the apparent escalation on 2025-11-29, which sounded like it could have involved aggressive or unsafe behavior. The combination of loud music, intoxicated shouting, and possible property damage or throwing of objects makes me uneasy about potential safety issues in the building.

I am requesting that management formally address this situation with the resident of A-202. Ideally, I would like to see a written notice reminding them of quiet hours and noise policies, along with clear expectations about overnight guests and the volume of music, especially after 10:00 PM on weeknights and 11:00 PM on weekends. If possible, I would also appreciate being informed of any steps that are taken so I know the issue is being taken seriously.

If needed, I am willing to provide the noise log I have been keeping with dates and times, as well as copies of text messages exchanged with my upstairs neighbor, Sarah Kim, confirming that she has also been disturbed by the noise from A-202. I am also willing to speak with management by phone or in person to clarify any details or answer questions about the incidents described above.

My goal is not to create unnecessary conflict with my neighbor but to restore a reasonable level of quiet and safety in the building during nighttime hours. I have tried to address the situation informally once already and have given it a few months to see if it would improve, but it has not. For these reasons, I am now asking for formal assistance through this complaint notice.
